methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.2x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
SiPearl is a Series A chip design company in the Chips & Semiconductors sector, having raised €130M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 4 same-stage private peers in semiconductors (Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI) valued between $500M and $5.0B, alongside 3 public semiconductor benchmarks (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) with a median price-to-sales multiple of 24.2x.

The peer-median method is the applicable triangulation approach, given SiPearl's private status and undisclosed revenue. The median valuation across the 4 private comparables is $1.5B, with a floor of $500M (Rain AI) and ceiling of $5.0B (Etched). This method directly reflects market outcomes for companies at identical stage and sector. Public comps anchoring cannot be applied without SiPearl's revenue, a data point rarely disclosed by private firms.

The triangulated valuation range is $1.3B to $1.7B, centered on the peer median of $1.5B. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method and a modest peer set of four companies. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the peer cohort—particularly whether Etched and d-Matrix at the high end ($2.0B–$5.0B) accurately proxy SiPearl's position—and SiPearl's revenue trajectory relative to peers, which cannot be assessed from available data.
